phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your clone-root deception beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — clone-root deception edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your clone-root deception beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — clone-root deception edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Pop Blossom online — the disguise plus R turns any clump into a 3-second knockup ambush.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your clone-root deception in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Anivia's wall-frost poke control own this window — your clone-root deception don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-poke the last-hits — the triple bloom on a rooted or low target already stings at one.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the clone walks one way, you walk the other — the lane's information war starts now.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the kill pattern: root into full bloom is half a health bar with ignite from level 3.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your clone-root deception out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your clone-root deception beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: root-bloom deletes squishies and the clone sells the angle. Force the contact war.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Neeko is an ambush: disguise into their formation, R the clump, and root whatever scatters.

phases.why: Pop Blossom online — the disguise plus R turns any clump into a 3-second knockup ambush.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: root-bloom deletes squishies and the clone sells the angle. Force the contact war.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Neeko is an ambush: disguise into their formation, R the clump, and root whatever scatters.
